tenors.narrative: Prediction markets show minimal conviction near current levels, with a median of $76,661 (-0.3%) across 42 markets. The 10th-90th percentile range of $74,762-$79,224 reflects modest near-term volatility expectations, with roughly equal probability mass above and below spot.

Markets price a modest 1% rally to $77,632 by April 3rd, though with wider dispersion than the prior day. The interquartile range of $75,301-$79,963 and 90th percentile at $81,361 suggest increased tail risk to the upside despite light trading volumes.

Year-end 2026 predictions show extreme uncertainty with no active markets and a median of $59,000 (-23.2%), but a massive $40,000-$191,667 range indicates the market assigns significant probability to both major downside (10th percentile $40,000) and explosive upside (90th percentile $191,667) scenarios.

tenors.narrative: Prediction markets expect WTI to trade near $96/bbl by end-April 2026, representing a modest 3.1% decline from current levels. With 19 active markets and substantial $3.9M volume, there is relatively tight consensus around this median, though the 10th-90th percentile range of $78-$113 reflects meaningful uncertainty about intermediate-term fundamentals.

By end-June 2026, prediction markets show materially weaker sentiment with a median of $78/bbl, implying a 20.9% decline from spot prices. The wide interquartile range ($68-$135) and elevated 90th percentile of $166 suggest substantial disagreement about longer-term supply-demand dynamics, despite lower trading volume at this tenor.

tenors.narrative: Prediction markets expect gold to decline 4.2% to a median of $4,430 by mid-2026, with moderate consensus reflected across 10 markets totaling $121k in volume. The 25th percentile sits at $4,200 while the 75th percentile reaches $4,582, indicating a wide dispersion of outcomes but with the distribution skewed toward weakness. Only 10% of the probability mass lies above $4,653, suggesting limited upside conviction beyond current spot levels.
